skill.name: pipedrive-automation
skill.description: Automate Pipedrive CRM workflows through Rube MCP, including deals, contacts, organizations, activities, notes, pipelines, and stages.
skill.summary: Guide AI assistants through Pipedrive CRM operations using Rube MCP tool sequences and schema checks.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command findings, but the cited lines are markdown references to Rube and Pipedrive MCP tools, not executable Ruby or shell code. The real risk is that the skill directs agents to connect to an external MCP endpoint and perform read and write operations against Pipedrive CRM data, so publication should include a data-access warning.

medium_findings.title: External MCP Access to CRM Data
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to add the Rube MCP endpoint and connect a Pipedrive account. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it enables network-mediated access to sensitive CRM records and write operations such as creating deals, contacts, activities, and notes.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network endpoint and CRM write workflows are explicitly documented. The behavior appears intended, but it can expose or modify business data if used with excessive permissions.

low_findings.title: Markdown Tool Names Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The repeated external command findings are false positives. The cited lines use markdown backticks to format MCP tool names and parameter names, and the skill file contains no executable shell, Ruby, or script blocks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of the cited lines shows documentation-only inline code formatting. No command interpreter, script body, or execution instruction is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm findings do not identify cryptographic code. The cited lines are the frontmatter description and a Pipedrive response field note, with no hashing, encryption, or signature logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact contexts are natural-language documentation about Pipedrive workflows and response fields. There is no crypto API usage or algorithm selection to exploit.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Match Is a False Positive
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance finding points to a Pipedrive tool sequence item for creating a deal. It does not instruct host inspection, environment discovery, or local system probing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line names a CRM action and required deal fields. No local machine or network reconnaissance behavior is described.

dangerous_patterns.title: Third-Party MCP Endpoint Configuration
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill asks users to add an external MCP server URL. This is not malicious by itself, but users should trust the provider and understand that CRM data may flow through the MCP connection.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded endpoint is explicitly present and is central to the skill. The risk depends on user consent and the MCP provider security posture.

content.value_statement: Sales teams spend time translating CRM requests into exact Pipedrive API steps. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured Rube MCP workflows for common Pipedrive tasks.
actual_capabilities: Guides agents to verify Rube MCP and connect a Pipedrive account before running workflows.
Provides ordered tool sequences for creating and updating deals, contacts, organizations, activities, and notes.
Explains how to resolve pipeline, stage, person, organization, and deal identifiers before write operations.
Documents key Pipedrive parameters, including email arrays, activity status values, visibility settings, and pagination fields.
Lists common pitfalls for search, custom fields, soft deletes, response nesting, rate limits, and stage summaries.
limitations: Requires an active Rube MCP connection and an authorized Pipedrive account.
Does not execute Pipedrive actions by itself; it guides an assistant to use available MCP tools.
Tool schemas can change, so the skill instructs agents to search current schemas first.
Bulk CRM changes need user review because the workflows can create or update live business records.

use_cases.title: Prepare Sales Deal Entries
use_cases.description: Create a new deal, link it to the correct contact and company, choose the pipeline stage, and add expected value.

use_cases.title: Maintain CRM Contact Quality
use_cases.description: Search for existing people and organizations before creating records, then update details without introducing duplicates.

use_cases.title: Schedule Follow-Up Work
use_cases.description: Create calls, meetings, tasks, and notes linked to the right deal, person, or organization.

prompt_templates.title: Check My Pipedrive Connection
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Pipedrive automation skill to verify that Rube MCP is available, check whether my Pipedrive connection is active, and tell me what is needed before making CRM changes.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Qualified Deal
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Pipedrive tools to create a deal for [company] with [contact], value [amount], currency [currency], expected close date [date], and stage [stage name]. Search for existing records first.

prompt_templates.title: Update Contacts and Add Follow-Up
prompt_templates.prompt: Find [person] at [organization], update their email and phone if needed, then create a follow-up activity for [date and time] linked to the relevant open deal.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Pipeline Segment
prompt_templates.prompt: List deals in [pipeline name] and [stage name], paginate as needed, summarize stale opportunities, and propose safe updates for my approval before changing records.

output_examples.input: Create a deal for Acme with Jane Lee, worth 25000 USD, in the Enterprise pipeline.
output_examples.output: The assistant searches for Acme and Jane Lee, resolves the Enterprise pipeline and stage, then asks for approval before creating the deal.

output_examples.input: Add a note to the renewal deal that the customer requested procurement terms.
output_examples.output: The assistant finds the matching renewal deal, confirms the record, and adds a Pipedrive note linked to that deal.

output_examples.input: Show all open activities for a sales user and mark completed calls as done.
output_examples.output: The assistant lists assigned activities, identifies call tasks, and requests confirmation before updating completion status.

best_practices: Search current Rube MCP tool schemas before using any Pipedrive operation.
Resolve names to numeric Pipedrive identifiers before creating or updating related records.
Ask for user confirmation before bulk updates, destructive changes, or changes to deal status.
anti_patterns: Do not create new people or organizations before checking for existing matching records.
Do not assume visible names are valid IDs for deals, stages, pipelines, contacts, or organizations.
Do not run broad list operations without pagination limits or a clear business purpose.

faq.question: Does this skill require a Pipedrive API key?
faq.answer: No direct API key is described. It requires Rube MCP and an active Pipedrive OAuth connection through that MCP server.

faq.question: Can it create and update live CRM records?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented workflows include creating and updating deals, people, organizations, activities, and notes.

faq.question: Why should the assistant search tools first?
faq.answer: Pipedrive tool schemas can change. Searching first helps the assistant use current parameters and avoid invalid requests.

faq.question: Can it handle custom Pipedrive fields?
faq.answer: It notes that custom fields appear as hash-like keys and should be mapped through the relevant fields endpoint.

faq.question: Is this safe for bulk CRM cleanup?
faq.answer: Use caution. The skill documents pagination and rate limits, but bulk changes should be reviewed and approved before execution.

faq.question: What tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code when the required MCP tooling is available.
